rationale: Trump's statement about ships moving through Hormuz is rhetoric without confirmed new action; the DRC Ebola situation continues without US risk escalation; no domestic events this cycle rise above background. Score adjusted down: ongoing situation without qualitative change.

top_events.title: Trump says ships 'starting to move' through Strait of Hormuz
top_events.summary: Trump's claim about Hormuz is rhetoric — no confirmed new shipping data or policy action. The existing deal is already priced in; no new impact on US oil markets or daily life.

top_events.title: Record jump in DRC Ebola outbreak takes death toll to 181
top_events.summary: DRC Ebola death toll rises to 181 — US experts confirm risk remains extremely low and preparedness is in place. Continuation of monitored situation with no US escalation.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic at conference for 'espionage activities'
top_events.summary: Another US academic detained by China — continuation of an established pattern of detentions. Relevant to US-China tensions but no new qualitative escalation.
